channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: LLM Cost Attribution with OTel, Next.js for AI Agents, LLM Security Testing

item.title: SQLite Optimizer Deep Dive, Change-Set Internals & Azure PostgreSQL Architecture

item.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
item.description: With just two weeks to go, StrictlyVC Los Angeles is quickly approaching. On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus in El Segundo, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather...

item.title: Should AI Help Write the Tests, or Change What You Test?
item.description: You just merged an AI-assisted feature branch, the code review looks clean, and the app works in your local smoke test. Now comes the real question: do you add another traditional browser test,...

item.title: Defender zero-days CVE-2026-41091 and 45498 — what defenders should do today (May 2026)
item.description: Microsoft published two Defender vulnerabilities on May 19, 2026 that are being actively exploited in the wild, and CISA has already pushed both into the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog....

item.title: Is AI CAD the Future Or Is It Already Here?

item.description: You sold performance monitoring on the proposal, and the contract is signed. Now delivery has three days before someone asks why nothing is live yet. Onboarding is where many retainers wobble....

item.title: Ghostty: A GPU-Accelerated Terminal That Works With Zero Config
item.description: Ghostty: A GPU-Accelerated Terminal That Works With Zero Config Ghostty is a terminal emulator built by Mitchell Hashimoto, the founder of HashiCorp. Its goal is to nail speed, a rich feature set,...

item.title: AI tool evaluation framework
item.description: The Honest AI Tool Evaluation Framework Nobody Is Writing Last October I had 14 AI tools running in parallel across three monitors. Cursor for code, Claude.ai for reasoning, Perplexity for...

item.title: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
item.description: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The...

item.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
item.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

item.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
item.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

item.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety: Customizable Multimodal Safety for Global Enterprise AI
The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked
item.description: On Uncanny Valley, we dive into the IPO bonanza that the top AI companies are embarking on to the point where some real estate listings are looking for not just regular old cash, but Anthropic stock.

item.title: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
item.description: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4. The Shark...

item.title: Google is letting social media stars customize their search result page
item.description: Google now lets big creators and publishers in the US claim dedicated profiles in Search to highlight things like videos, articles, and their other profiles online. But this feature won't be...

item.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
item.description: Amazon's gaming strategy has never really been clear. It's been very active in the space: acquiring Twitch, launching its Luna cloud gaming service nearly six years ago, investing heavily in MMOs...

item.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
item.description: Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root. It is tracked as...

item.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
item.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and...

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: Belkin’s new Joy-Con grips also boost the Switch 2’s battery life

item.title: Cash App made a magic wand for contactless payments

item.title: Windows is back on the Microsoft menu
item.description: I can't remember the last time Microsoft kicked off a Build keynote with Windows front and center, but that's exactly what CEO Satya Nadella did this week. Nadella didn't address the issues...

item.title: Remarkable’s refurbished Paper Pro bundle is hundreds off
item.description: Color E Ink tablets aren’t usually affordable. I’m not going to say that Woot’s price on a refurbished “good as new” Remarkable Paper Pro is cheap, but it’s pretty fantastic compared to buying one...

item.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
item.description: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub...

item.title: Agentic AI Is Transforming Defense, But Only Secure IT Infrastructure Will Maximize It

item.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
item.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

item.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
